steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The phrase “—including the ______ Madurese and Bikol, which…—” sits inside a pair of em-dashes that already marks it as a parenthetical. “Including” is a preposition; with choice A, its object (“the languages Madurese and Bikol”) follows directly. Rule: no punctuation may interrupt a preposition from its object.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — languages: No added punctuation — “including” flows directly into its object. Correct. ✓
  • Choice B — languages—: A second em-dash inside a parenthetical already bounded by em-dashes creates an unmatched, conflicting boundary. Violates em-dash pairing rule. ✗
  • Choice C — languages:: A colon must follow an independent clause; “including the languages” is a prepositional phrase, not an independent clause. Violates colon rule. ✗
  • Choice D — languages,: A comma between the preposition “including” and its object interrupts the prepositional phrase without cause. Violates comma rule. ✗
That is the correct answer.
